data.body:
[PASS 1 review — T3 Integration feasibility — S4 attested signing]

AFFIRMATIONS (T3 criteria this slice satisfies):
- technical_feasibility / named-internal-API existence — MATCH (verified against current source). The core feasibility claim — that the SHA can be bound through the EXISTING manifest channel WITHOUT a payload-schema/version change — checks out exactly against signing.py:
  • `signing._canonical_payload` signs `{v, algorithm, ticket_id, manifest}` (src/rebar/signing.py:188-201) — confirmed.
  • `compute_signature` signs over the full `manifest: list[str]` (signing.py:204-206) — confirmed, so a `verified-at-sha:<sha>` MANIFEST STEP enters the signed bytes without touching `_canonical_payload`.
  • `PAYLOAD_VERSION = 1` (signing.py:56) is referenced by the canonical payload (signing.py:196); adding a manifest step does NOT alter `v`, so AC1's "no PAYLOAD_VERSION bump / prior signatures still verify" is technically sound and the regression-test obligation is well-targeted.
  • The `head_sha` audit field exists today as audit-only metadata (signing.py:24, :243, :354; head_sha() at signing.py:300) — confirming the plan's "keep head_sha as is (additive)" / "nothing cryptographically pins WHAT code was verified" premise.
  This is an INTERNAL, already-used integration extended additively — T3 largely not-applicable to the signing channel itself; it PASSES.
- The "in-toto Statement shape so HMAC→DSSE is an envelope swap" (AC6) is a forward-compat data-shape contract, not a dependency on an external in-toto/DSSE library at build time — no unverified external capability introduced now.

No T3 finding for this slice. (The standing cross-child concern about routing the pinned-SHA snapshot handle into plan_review/attest.py's dependency_hashes()/_rehash()/freshness re-check is a G4 consistency/dependency-gap seam already filed on this ticket — out of T3 scope; it is not an external-integration-feasibility issue.)
